# Align the Ollama + OpenAiCompatible interactive-timeout contracts

## Context

Follow-up to PR #12806 (@tobiu-requested). #12806 gave `Ollama.generate` an interactive `timeoutMs` (it had none ‚Äî it hardcoded a 1h socket timeout). `OpenAiCompatible.generate` already had its own timeout handling. The two chat providers are parallel implementations, and #12806 ‚Äî while fixing the Ollama gap ‚Äî left their timeout **contracts** diverged. They need to stay in sync.

Live latest-open sweep: checked the latest 20 open issues at 2026-06-09T08:35Z; no equivalent open ticket (the matches ‚Äî #12803, #12487, #12509, #12742 ‚Äî are all closed).

## The Problem

A caller (`SearchService.ask` today; a daemon-yield path later) gets **different** timeout behavior depending on which provider is active. Two confirmed divergences (V-B-A'd at the source):

1. **Error shape ‚Äî a caller cannot uniformly detect a provider timeout.** `OpenAiCompatible` throws a structured error with `error.code = 'OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_TIMEOUT'` (`ai/provider/OpenAiCompatible.mjs:16-18`). `Ollama` (post-#12806) throws a **plain `Error`** (`[Ollama] ‚Ä¶ timed out after Nms (host=, model=)`) with **no `.code`**. A caller checking `err.code` sees one provider's timeout and not the other's.
2. **`options.signal` (upstream cancellation).** `OpenAiCompatible` honors it ‚Äî full AbortSignal wiring (`OpenAiCompatible.mjs:257-277`: adopts an upstream signal into the `AbortController`). `Ollama` **strips** `options.signal` (`Ollama.mjs` payload-prep) and ignores upstream cancellation. (This is the signal-parity item deliberately deferred out of #12806 ‚Äî see the #12803 AC-correction, `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABFZtiXg`.)

The message format is already roughly parallel (`[Provider] {label} timed out after {ms}ms (host=, model=)`), so the gap is the detectable code + signal support ‚Äî and, structurally, the absence of a shared contract that would keep the two from drifting further as either evolves.

## The Architectural Reality

- **`ai/provider/OpenAiCompatible.mjs`** ‚Äî `createTimeoutError({operationLabel, timeoutMs, host, modelName})` ‚Üí `error.code = 'OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_TIMEOUT'` (`:16-18`); upstream-signal adoption + timeout `AbortController` (`:257-280`); transport is `fetch` (`controller.signal` ‚Üí `fetch`).
- **`ai/provider/Ollama.mjs`** (post-#12806) ‚Äî socket timeout via `req.on('timeout')` ‚Üí `req.destroy()` + a plain `Error` (no `.code`); `options.signal` stripped in payload-prep; transport is node `http.request`.
- Both consumed via `ai/provider/buildChatModel.mjs` ‚Üí `SearchService.ask`; a future signal-consumer is the LEAF-2 daemon-yield (#12799).

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Ollama.generate(options.signal)` | this ticket | honor the upstream `AbortSignal` (node `http.request` accepts `signal` natively ‚Üí destroy on abort) | current: stripped/ignored | `ai/provider/Ollama.mjs` | `OpenAiCompatible.mjs:257-277` is the reference impl |
| `{Ollama,OpenAiCompatible}.generate` timeout error | this ticket | uniform **detectable** shape ‚Äî a shared `.code` (or shared error type) + the structured fields | current: per-provider (`.code` only on OpenAiCompatible) | `ai/provider/*.mjs` | `OpenAiCompatible.mjs:16-18` (`'OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_TIMEOUT'`) is the precedent to generalize |

## Decision Record impact

`none` (provider-internal contract alignment; no ADR governs the provider abstraction surface today).

## The Fix

Align both providers on one interactive-timeout **contract**:
1. **Signal parity** ‚Äî `Ollama.generate` honors `options.signal` (wire it to `http.request`'s native `signal`; destroy + throw the timeout-shaped error on abort).
2. **Detectable uniform error** ‚Äî a shared timeout-error helper (one `.code`, e.g. `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT`, or a shared error type) so callers detect a provider timeout regardless of which provider served the call. Generalize `createTimeoutError` rather than duplicate it.
3. **Sync by construction** ‚Äî house the shared error helper + a documented `generate(options)` contract (`timeoutMs`, `signal`, `operationLabel`) in one place so the providers can't silently drift again. The transport mechanism may differ (`fetch`+`AbortController` vs `http.request`); only the observable contract must be uniform.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Ollama.generate` honors `options.signal` (upstream abort ‚Üí request destroyed ‚Üí timeout-shaped error), matching `OpenAiCompatible`.
- [ ] Both providers throw a timeout error with a **uniform, caller-detectable** shape (shared `.code` or shared type), so `SearchService` (and future consumers) can branch on a provider timeout without per-provider knowledge.
- [ ] The shared timeout-error shape lives in one helper (generalized from `createTimeoutError`); the `generate(options)` timeout/signal contract is documented at the shared site.
- [ ] Provider-level regressions for **both** providers (the new `signal` abort + the uniform error shape) ‚Äî extend `KeepAlive.spec.mjs` (hung loopback for Ollama; `fetch`-mock for OpenAiCompatible).

## Out of Scope

- The `ask_knowledge_base` synthesis-timeout wiring (#12806 ‚Äî done).
- The daemon-yield itself (#12799 LEAF 2 ‚Äî a future *consumer* of `signal`, not this contract work).
- Other provider-parity dimensions (`keep_alive`, streaming) ‚Äî already aligned or separately tracked.

## Avoided Traps

- **Not** forcing identical mechanisms ‚Äî `fetch`+`AbortController` vs `http.request` socket timeout may differ; only the observable contract (error shape + signal honoring) must be uniform.
- **Not** speculative over-abstraction ‚Äî a shared helper is justified by a concrete, demonstrated divergence (#12806) and an operator "stay in sync" directive, not by gold-plating a single-provider path.

## Related

#12806 (introduced the divergence), #12803 (origin ticket + signal-deferral AC-correction `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABFZtiXg`), #12799 (LEAF 2 ‚Äî likely first `signal` consumer), #10494, #12740 (local-first provider defaults epic).

- 2026-06-09T10:41:26Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `4a6d405` - "fix(ai): align Ollama + OpenAiCompatible interactive-timeout contracts (#12814)

Post-#12806 the two local chat providers' timeout contracts diverged: Ollama.generate
threw a plain Error with no .code (vs OpenAiCompatible's structured error.code) and
stripped options.signal (vs OpenAiCompatible's full AbortSignal adoption) ‚Äî so a caller
could neither uniformly detect a provider timeout nor cancel an in-flight Ollama request
(the latter being the first consumer LEAF-2 daemon-yield will need).

- Extract createTimeoutError into a shared ai/provider/createTimeoutError.mjs with a
  uniform caller-detectable code (PROVIDER_TIMEOUT) plus provider/timeoutMs fields and the
  documented generate(options) contract, so the two parallel providers cannot re-drift.
- Ollama.generate now honors options.signal (wired natively into http.request, destroy on
  abort) and throws the shared timeout-error shape.
- OpenAiCompatible adopts the shared helper (message + behavior unchanged; code unified
  from OPENAI_COMPATIBLE_TIMEOUT to PROVIDER_TIMEOUT).
- KeepAlive.spec: assert the uniform code/provider on both providers' timeouts plus a new
  Ollama signal-abort regression.

Transport stays per-provider (fetch+AbortController vs http.request); only the observable
contract is unified. No consumer branched on the old code (verified repo-wide). 12/12
KeepAlive specs pass."
